from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, trim, lit, upper, when

# This list should be validated with latest one
# Please check this link for latest information 
# https://www.acm.org/publications/openaccess
fully_open_access = ["TACO","THRI","TOPLAS","TOPML","JDS","COLA","CACM","DGOV","DTRAP","FAC","ICPS","PACMPL","PACMSE"]

# The default value should be validated with latest one
# Plese check theis link for latest default APC
# https://www.acm.org/publications/openaccess#oapricing
default_apc = 1800
Year = 2025

kbart_temp_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/bronze/Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)/kbart/*.txt'
silver_path = 'abfss://apc@dloadatdl.dfs.core.windows.net/silver/Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)/all/'

# Step 1: Read the file into a PySpark DataFrame, specifying the delimiter as '\t'
kbart_df = spark.read.option("delimiter", "\t").csv(kbart_temp_path, header=True, inferSchema=True)

# Remove rows where publication_type is not "serial".
# Ensure last_date_online is blank (null).
# Ensure publisher is not "ACM".

filtered_kbart_df = kbart_df.filter(
    (col("publication_type") == "Serial") &
    (col("publisher_name") == "Association for Computing Machinery") &
    ((trim(col("date_last_issue_online")).isNull()) | (trim(col("date_last_issue_online")) == ""))
)

new_df = filtered_kbart_df.select(
    lit("Association for Computing Machinery (ACM)").alias("Publisher"),
    col("print_identifier").alias("ISSN1"),
    col("online_identifier").alias("ISSN2"),
    col("publication_title").alias("Title"),
    lit("Publisher Website via Misc Publisher APCs list").alias("Source"),
    #lit("").alias("Model"),
    when(upper(col("title_id")).isin(fully_open_access), "Fully OA").otherwise("Hybrid").alias("Model"),
    lit(default_apc).alias("APC"),
    lit("USD").alias("Currency"),
    lit("").alias("License Types"),
    lit("").alias("Imprint"),
    lit("").alias("Model Notes"),
    lit(Year).alias("Year"),
    col("title_id").alias("PublisherID"),
    col("title_id").alias("JoiningID"),
    col("title_url").alias("Publisher URL"),
    col("publisher_name").alias("Society")

desired_columns = [
    "Publisher", "ISSN1", "ISSN2", "Title", "Source", "Model", "Model Notes",
    "APC", "Currency", "License Types", "Year", "PublisherID", "JoiningID",
    "Imprint", "Society"
]

# Reorder the columns based on desired schema
df_reordered = new_df.select([col for col in desired_columns if col in new_df.columns])
